steps.title: Use the first two years
steps.content: At $x=0$, the house is worth $174{,}000$. A $2.4\%$ yearly increase means multiply by $1.024$, so after one year the value is $174{,}000(1.024)=178{,}176$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: In $y=a(b)^x$, $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the multiplier for each year. Here $a=174{,}000$ and $b=1.024$, so the model is $y=174{,}000(1.024)^x$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The choices with multipliers less than $1$ model decreases. The model with $1.24$ represents a $24%$ increase, not a $2.4%$ increase. The model $f(x)=174{,}000(1.024)^x$ uses the correct growth multiplier, so C is correct.
